summary: Add the Run Scan API and scan orchestrator core chain with mock SERP, deterministic agent fallbacks, scoring, persistence-ready run records, and focused tests.
acceptance_criteria: A scan orchestrator module exists at services/scan-orchestrator.ts with a stable runScan() entry point and typed input/output contracts for radarTaskId, useMockSerp, optional limits, run status, persisted counts, errors, and generated opportunities.

The orchestrator loads an active RadarTask, creates a SearchRun, then runs the core chain in order: keyword expansion, SERP provider search, SERP analysis, opportunity analysis, calculateOpportunityScore(), and persistence of keyword candidates, SERP results, opportunities, scoreBreakdown, scoreExplanation, and final run counts/status.

The default path is fully local and deterministic when no LLM client or real SERP provider is supplied: useMockSerp defaults to true, MockSerpProvider is used, agent heuristic fallbacks run without API keys, and no network or paid service is required.

The orchestrator records completed, failed, or partial_failed run status and sanitized error messages; a single keyword failure does not discard successful opportunities when partial success is possible.
A POST /api/scans/run route exists, validates JSON input, calls runScan(), and returns structured JSON responses with sanitized validation/not-found/runtime errors consistent with existing API style.

Focused automated tests cover successful mock scan orchestration, persistence payloads including score breakdown/explanation, invalid or inactive radar task handling, partial failure behavior, API validation/response shape where practical, and no-secret/no-network defaults.
README documents the Run Scan API/orchestrator slice, local mock behavior, request shape, response shape, validation commands, and explicit non-goals.

No dashboard, Radar detail UI, opportunity list/detail UI, cron, auth/session, real SERP provider, real LLM provider setup, environment secret loading, deployment, schema migration, or package dependency change is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/09_codex_implementation_tasks.md Task 11 and docs/11_development_plan.md M4 Run Scan core chain.
Prisma schema already contains SearchRun, KeywordCandidate, SerpResult, and Opportunity tables; this task should not alter schema unless an implementation blocker proves otherwise.
systems_architect is required because this connects agents, providers, scoring, API route, and persistence boundaries.
domain_designer is required because this decides how opportunities are saved, scored, and surfaced from risk-aware analysis.
security_reviewer is required because this adds a mutating POST API route and database writes, even though MVP remains local unauthenticated admin.
Tests should prefer dependency injection/fakes for the orchestrator where possible while keeping the default production path wired to Prisma and mock SERP.
non_goals: No UI pages or components: no dashboard, Radar detail button, opportunity list, opportunity detail, score badge, or recent run status component.
No real SERP provider or real LLM provider configuration, no process.env secret loading, no paid service setup, and no network calls in tests.
No Prisma schema edits, migrations, seed changes, package or lockfile changes unless a blocker is documented first.
No cron/daily scan, background jobs, queues, email digest, auth/session, multi-user enforcement, deployment config, or GitHub Actions changes.
No MVP Spec Agent or opportunity MVP spec generation.
No changes to existing agent contracts, scoring formula, SERP provider contracts, LLM client contracts, or Radar Task CRUD API unless strictly necessary for type-only consumption.
